req.title: Beneficiary management (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Beneficiary Cooling-Off Period Requirement

```python
"""
Test Suite: Beneficiary Cooling-Off Period
Requirement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour
cooling-off period before it can receive transfers.
Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from uuid import uuid4

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / service interfaces assumed to exist in production code.
# Adjust import paths to match your actual project structure.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from banking.beneficiary import BeneficiaryService, Beneficiary, BeneficiaryStatus
from banking.transfer import TransferService, TransferRequest, TransferResult
from banking.exceptions import (
    CoolingOffPeriodError,
    BeneficiaryNotFoundError,
    DuplicateBeneficiaryError,
    InvalidBeneficiaryError,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
COOLING_OFF_HOURS = 24
COOLING_OFF_SECONDS = COOLING_OFF_HOURS * 3600  # 86 400

CUSTOMER_ID = "CUST-001"
ACCOUNT_NUMBER = "GB29NWBK60161331926819"
SORT_CODE = "60-16-13"
BENEFICIARY_NAME = "Jane Doe"


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================


@pytest.fixture
def mock_clock():
    """Controllable UTC clock injected into services."""
    clock = MagicMock()
    clock.utcnow.return_value = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    return clock


@pytest.fixture
def mock_repo():
    """In-memory beneficiary repository stub."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_log():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_notification():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def beneficiary_service(mock_repo, mock_clock, mock_audit_log, mock_notification):
    return BeneficiaryService(
        repository=mock_repo,
        clock=mock_clock,
        audit_log=mock_audit_log,
        notification_service=mock_notification,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def transfer_service(mock_repo, mock_clock):
    return TransferService(repository=mock_repo, clock=mock_clock)


@pytest.fixture
def added_beneficiary(mock_clock):
    """A beneficiary whose cooling-off period started at the mocked 'now'."""
    return Beneficiary(
        beneficiary_id=str(uuid4()),
        customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
        name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
        account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
        sort_code=SORT_CODE,
        status=BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF,
        added_at=mock_clock.utcnow(),
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. ADDING A BENEFICIARY
# ===========================================================================


class TestAddBeneficiary:

    def test_add_valid_beneficiary_returns_beneficiary_object(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo
    ):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = None
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        result = beneficiary_service.add(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
            account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
        )

        assert isinstance(result, Beneficiary)
        assert result.customer_id == CUSTOMER_ID
        assert result.account_number == ACCOUNT_NUMBER

    def test_new_beneficiary_status_is_cooling_off(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo
    ):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = None
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        result = beneficiary_service.add(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
            account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
        )

        assert result.status == BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF

    def test_new_beneficiary_added_at_is_set_to_current_utc_time(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = None
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b
        expected_time = mock_clock.utcnow()

        result = beneficiary_service.add(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
            account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
        )

        assert result.added_at == expected_time

    def test_add_beneficiary_persists_to_repository(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo
    ):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = None
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        beneficiary_service.add(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
            account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
        )

        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_add_beneficiary_sends_confirmation_notification(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, mock_notification
    ):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = None
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        beneficiary_service.add(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
            account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
        )

        mock_notification.send_cooling_off_notification.assert_called_once()

    def test_add_beneficiary_writes_audit_log(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, mock_audit_log
    ):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = None
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        beneficiary_service.add(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
            account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
        )

        mock_audit_log.record.assert_called_once()

    def test_add_duplicate_beneficiary_raises_duplicate_error(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary
    ):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = added_beneficiary

        with pytest.raises(DuplicateBeneficiaryError):
            beneficiary_service.add(
                customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
                name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
                account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
                sort_code=SORT_CODE,
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "name, account_number, sort_code",
        [
            ("", ACCOUNT_NUMBER, SORT_CODE),          # empty name
            (None, ACCOUNT_NUMBER, SORT_CODE),         # null name
            (BENEFICIARY_NAME, "", SORT_CODE),          # empty account
            (BENEFICIARY_NAME, "INVALID_ACC", SORT_CODE),  # bad account format
            (BENEFICIARY_NAME, ACCOUNT_NUMBER, ""),    # empty sort code
            (BENEFICIARY_NAME, ACCOUNT_NUMBER, "99-99-99"),  # invalid sort code
        ],
    )
    def test_add_beneficiary_with_invalid_inputs_raises_invalid_error(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, name, account_number, sort_code
    ):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(InvalidBeneficiaryError):
            beneficiary_service.add(
                customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
                name=name,
                account_number=account_number,
                sort_code=sort_code,
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 2. COOLING-OFF PERIOD STATUS CHECKS  (core logic)
# ===========================================================================


class TestCoolingOffStatus:

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 2a. is_cooling_off() helper / property
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_beneficiary_is_in_cooling_off_immediately_after_adding(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        elapsed = timedelta(seconds=0)
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at + elapsed

        assert added_beneficiary.is_cooling_off(mock_clock.utcnow()) is True

    def test_beneficiary_is_in_cooling_off_one_second_after_adding(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=1)

        assert added_beneficiary.is_cooling_off(mock_clock.utcnow()) is True

    def test_beneficiary_is_in_cooling_off_at_23_hours_59_minutes_59_seconds(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        """One second before the cooling-off period expires — still blocked."""
        one_second_before_expiry = timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS - 1)
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at + one_second_before_expiry

        assert added_beneficiary.is_cooling_off(mock_clock.utcnow()) is True

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # BOUNDARY: exact 24-hour mark
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_beneficiary_cooling_off_expires_at_exactly_24_hours(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        """At t=86400s the cooling-off period is over; transfers must be allowed."""
        exactly_24h = timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS)
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at + exactly_24h

        assert added_beneficiary.is_cooling_off(mock_clock.utcnow()) is False

    def test_beneficiary_cooling_off_not_expired_one_millisecond_before_24_hours(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        """t = 86399.999s — still within the cooling-off period."""
        just_before = timedelta(milliseconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS * 1000 - 1)
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at + just_before

        assert added_beneficiary.is_cooling_off(mock_clock.utcnow()) is True

    def test_beneficiary_cooling_off_expired_one_second_after_24_hours(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        """t = 86401s — safely past the cooling-off period."""
        one_second_after = timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS + 1)
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at + one_second_after

        assert added_beneficiary.is_cooling_off(mock_clock.utcnow()) is False

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 2b. remaining_cooling_off_seconds()
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_remaining_cooling_off_at_t0_is_86400_seconds(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at
        remaining = added_beneficiary.remaining_cooling_off_seconds(mock_clock.utcnow())

        assert remaining == COOLING_OFF_SECONDS

    def test_remaining_cooling_off_at_t_minus_1_second_is_1(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS - 1)
        )
        remaining = added_beneficiary.remaining_cooling_off_seconds(mock_clock.utcnow())

        assert remaining == 1

    def test_remaining_cooling_off_at_exactly_24h_is_zero(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS)
        )
        remaining = added_beneficiary.remaining_cooling_off_seconds(mock_clock.utcnow())

        assert remaining == 0

    def test_remaining_cooling_off_after_expiry_is_zero_not_negative(
        self, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        """Remaining time must never be reported as negative."""
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS + 3600)
        )
        remaining = added_beneficiary.remaining_cooling_off_seconds(mock_clock.utcnow())

        assert remaining == 0

    def test_cooling_off_expiry_timestamp_is_added_at_plus_24_hours(
        self, added_beneficiary
    ):
        expected_expiry = added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(hours=COOLING_OFF_HOURS)

        assert added_beneficiary.cooling_off_expires_at == expected_expiry


# ===========================================================================
# 3. TRANSFER BLOCKED DURING COOLING-OFF PERIOD
# ===========================================================================


class TestTransferBlockedDuringCoolingOff:

    def _make_transfer_request(self, beneficiary_id: str, amount: float = 100.0):
        return TransferRequest(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            beneficiary_id=beneficiary_id,
            amount=amount,
            currency="GBP",
            reference="Invoice 42",
        )

    def test_transfer_blocked_immediately_after_beneficiary_added(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at  # t=0

        with pytest.raises(CoolingOffPeriodError):
            transfer_service.initiate(
                self._make_transfer_request(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)
            )

    def test_transfer_blocked_one_second_into_cooling_off(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=1)

        with pytest.raises(CoolingOffPeriodError):
            transfer_service.initiate(
                self._make_transfer_request(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)
            )

    def test_transfer_blocked_at_23_hours_59_minutes_59_seconds(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS - 1)
        )

        with pytest.raises(CoolingOffPeriodError):
            transfer_service.initiate(
                self._make_transfer_request(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)
            )

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # BOUNDARY: exactly 24 hours — transfer MUST succeed
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_transfer_allowed_at_exactly_24_hours(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        added_beneficiary.status = BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS)
        )

        result = transfer_service.initiate(
            self._make_transfer_request(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)
        )

        assert isinstance(result, TransferResult)
        assert result.success is True

    def test_transfer_allowed_one_second_after_cooling_off_expires(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        added_beneficiary.status = BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS + 1)
        )

        result = transfer_service.initiate(
            self._make_transfer_request(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)
        )

        assert result.success is True

    def test_transfer_allowed_well_after_cooling_off_expires(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        added_beneficiary.status = BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(days=7)
        )

        result = transfer_service.initiate(
            self._make_transfer_request(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)
        )

        assert result.success is True

    def test_cooling_off_error_contains_remaining_seconds(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        """Error must surface how long the customer must wait."""
        elapsed = timedelta(seconds=3600)  # 1 hour in
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at + elapsed

        with pytest.raises(CoolingOffPeriodError) as exc_info:
            transfer_service.initiate(
                self._make_transfer_request(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)
            )

        assert exc_info.value.remaining_seconds == COOLING_OFF_SECONDS - 3600

    def test_cooling_off_error_contains_expiry_timestamp(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = added_beneficiary.added_at

        with pytest.raises(CoolingOffPeriodError) as exc_info:
            transfer_service.initiate(
                self._make_transfer_request(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)
            )

        expected_expiry = added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(hours=COOLING_OFF_HOURS)
        assert exc_info.value.expiry_time == expected_expiry

    def test_transfer_to_unknown_beneficiary_raises_not_found(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo
    ):
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(BeneficiaryNotFoundError):
            transfer_service.initiate(
                self._make_transfer_request("NON-EXISTENT-ID")
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 4. STATUS TRANSITION: COOLING_OFF → ACTIVE
# ===========================================================================


class TestCoolingOffStatusTransition:

    def test_status_transitions_to_active_after_24_hours(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS)
        )

        beneficiary = beneficiary_service.refresh_status(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)

        assert beneficiary.status == BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE

    def test_status_remains_cooling_off_before_24_hours(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS - 1)
        )

        beneficiary = beneficiary_service.refresh_status(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)

        assert beneficiary.status == BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF

    def test_status_transition_persisted_to_repository(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(hours=COOLING_OFF_HOURS)
        )

        beneficiary_service.refresh_status(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)

        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once()
        saved: Beneficiary = mock_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved.status == BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE

    def test_status_transition_audit_logged(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock, mock_audit_log
    ):
        mock_repo.get.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(hours=COOLING_OFF_HOURS)
        )

        beneficiary_service.refresh_status(added_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)

        mock_audit_log.record.assert_called()
        call_kwargs = mock_audit_log.record.call_args[1]
        assert call_kwargs.get("event") == "COOLING_OFF_EXPIRED"

    def test_active_beneficiary_does_not_revert_to_cooling_off(
        self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo, mock_clock
    ):
        active_beneficiary = Beneficiary(
            beneficiary_id=str(uuid4()),
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
            account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
            status=BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE,
            added_at=datetime(2024, 5, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc),  # long ago
        )
        mock_repo.get.return_value = active_beneficiary

        beneficiary = beneficiary_service.refresh_status(active_beneficiary.beneficiary_id)

        assert beneficiary.status == BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE


# ===========================================================================
# 5. MULTIPLE BENEFICIARIES — ISOLATION
# ===========================================================================


class TestMultipleBeneficiaries:

    def test_cooling_off_is_per_beneficiary_not_global(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, mock_clock
    ):
        now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = now

        cooling_off_bene = Beneficiary(
            beneficiary_id="BENE-001",
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name="Alice",
            account_number="GB11NWBK60161300000001",
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
            status=BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF,
            added_at=now - timedelta(hours=1),  # only 1 hour old
        )
        active_bene = Beneficiary(
            beneficiary_id="BENE-002",
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name="Bob",
            account_number="GB22NWBK60161300000002",
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
            status=BeneficiaryStatus.ACTIVE,
            added_at=now - timedelta(days=5),   # well past cooling off
        )

        def repo_side_effect(beneficiary_id):
            return cooling_off_bene if beneficiary_id == "BENE-001" else active_bene

        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.side_effect = repo_side_effect

        # Active beneficiary should succeed
        active_request = TransferRequest(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            beneficiary_id="BENE-002",
            amount=50.0,
            currency="GBP",
            reference="Ref-A",
        )
        result = transfer_service.initiate(active_request)
        assert result.success is True

        # Cooling-off beneficiary should be blocked
        blocked_request = TransferRequest(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            beneficiary_id="BENE-001",
            amount=50.0,
            currency="GBP",
            reference="Ref-B",
        )
        with pytest.raises(CoolingOffPeriodError):
            transfer_service.initiate(blocked_request)

    def test_two_beneficiaries_added_at_different_times_expire_independently(
        self, mock_clock
    ):
        t0 = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

        bene_early = Beneficiary(
            beneficiary_id="BENE-EARLY",
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name="Early Bird",
            account_number="GB33NWBK60161300000003",
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
            status=BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF,
            added_at=t0,
        )
        bene_late = Beneficiary(
            beneficiary_id="BENE-LATE",
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name="Late Comer",
            account_number="GB44NWBK60161300000004",
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
            status=BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF,
            added_at=t0 + timedelta(hours=12),
        )

        # At t0 + 24h: early is free, late is still cooling off
        check_time = t0 + timedelta(hours=24)

        assert bene_early.is_cooling_off(check_time) is False
        assert bene_late.is_cooling_off(check_time) is True


# ===========================================================================
# 6. EDGE CASES & CLOCK INTEGRITY
# ===========================================================================


class TestEdgeCases:

    def test_cooling_off_uses_utc_not_local_time(self, added_beneficiary):
        """Expiry must be calculated in UTC regardless of server timezone."""
        expiry = added_beneficiary.cooling_off_expires_at
        assert expiry.tzinfo == timezone.utc

    def test_added_at_is_timezone_aware(self, beneficiary_service, mock_repo):
        mock_repo.find_by_account.return_value = None
        mock_repo.save.side_effect = lambda b: b

        result = beneficiary_service.add(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name=BENEFICIARY_NAME,
            account_number=ACCOUNT_NUMBER,
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
        )

        assert result.added_at.tzinfo is not None

    def test_system_clock_cannot_be_manipulated_via_added_at(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, mock_clock
    ):
        """
        A beneficiary with a backdated added_at (e.g. set in the future
        so cooling-off appears expired) must be validated server-side.
        """
        now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = now

        # Attempt: added_at is set 25 hours in the past (cooling-off looks expired)
        # but the service must use its own authoritative clock, not trust client data.
        tampered_bene = Beneficiary(
            beneficiary_id=str(uuid4()),
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            name="Fraudster",
            account_number="GB55NWBK60161300000005",
            sort_code=SORT_CODE,
            status=BeneficiaryStatus.COOLING_OFF,   # server set COOLING_OFF
            added_at=now - timedelta(hours=1),       # only 1 hour old server-side
        )
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = tampered_bene

        with pytest.raises(CoolingOffPeriodError):
            transfer_service.initiate(
                TransferRequest(
                    customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
                    beneficiary_id=tampered_bene.beneficiary_id,
                    amount=1.0,
                    currency="GBP",
                    reference="Tamper-test",
                )
            )

    def test_cooling_off_period_boundary_is_inclusive_on_expiry_side(
        self, added_beneficiary
    ):
        """
        Boundary contract: is_cooling_off returns False when elapsed time
        is EXACTLY equal to the cooling-off duration (>=, not >).
        """
        exact_expiry = added_beneficiary.added_at + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS)
        result = added_beneficiary.is_cooling_off(exact_expiry)
        assert result is False, (
            "At exact expiry the cooling-off period should be over (>= boundary)"
        )

    def test_cooling_off_period_boundary_one_nanosecond_before_expiry(
        self, added_beneficiary
    ):
        """
        Python datetime resolution is microseconds; test at 1µs before expiry.
        """
        just_before = (
            added_beneficiary.added_at
            + timedelta(seconds=COOLING_OFF_SECONDS)
            - timedelta(microseconds=1)
        )
        assert added_beneficiary.is_cooling_off(just_before) is True

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("transfer_amount", [0.01, 1.00, 9999.99, 10_000.00])
    def test_transfer_amount_does_not_bypass_cooling_off(
        self, transfer_service, mock_repo, added_beneficiary, mock_clock, transfer_amount
    ):
        """The cooling-off block must apply regardless of transfer amount."""
        mock_repo.get_beneficiary.return_value = added_beneficiary
        mock_clock.utcnow.return_value = (
